name: After the assistant captures name + business, /settings shows them under Account + Business identity
description: Verifies the persistence path from HandleChatMessage's onboarding short-circuit through to the read side. /settings hits the composite /profile endpoint, which reads UserStore + BusinessIdentityStore. Captured values must surface in the read-only summary cards.

persona.name: Diego
persona.context: Wants to confirm what the app stored about him.

preconditions: On /assistant, has just replied 'Diego, Riley Roofing Co.' to the onboarding ask
Backend confirmed user.name='Diego', businessIdentity.businessName='Riley Roofing Co.'

steps.expect: /settings hero shows 'Riley Roofing Co.' as the display name (or 'Diego' as fallback when biz isn't set)
Name=Diego; Phone=+15125550100; Language=English (filled rows only — no 'Nothing set yet.' state)
Display name=Riley Roofing Co.; other fields (Legal name, Tagline, Website) show only if the user later sets them — empty fields are NOT rendered

steps.action: Returns to /assistant in a new conversation, sends 'Hey'
steps.expect: No onboarding ask fires (user.name is set); LLM handles the message normally

evaluationCriteria.check: Settings cards omit empty fields cleanly — no 'Email: —' rows; cards collapse to 'Nothing set yet.' only when ALL fields are unset
No 'edit' affordance on settings — copy reads 'Edits live in the assistant.' Clear that the assistant is the source of truth
/profile composite returns identity in the same request as user; cache invalidation isn't needed because settings page is islanded and refetches on mount
Card titles use semantic h3; data is in a definition-list-style grid with proper label/value association
